summary: [SYNTHETIC DATA] USDC pricing dislocation between Uniswap v3 and Curve StableSwap on Base. Synthetic observation: 18 bps edge detected in Curve->Uniswap direction with reasonable liquidity.
thesis: [SYNTHETIC] Curve Finance's StableSwap AMM for USDC-USDT pair is synthetically showing 18 bps worse price than Uniswap v3 for 50k USDC swap. This represents a temporary but executable dislocation if slippage and gas remain within synthetic estimates. Base network gas costs are low enough to make this viable for conservative capital.
liquidity_notes: [SYNTHETIC] Assumed ~$500k liquidity in Uniswap USDC/USDT 0.01% fee tier and $800k in Curve StableSwap pool. Synthetic routing: Buy 50k USDC on Curve (via USDT pair), swap back on Uniswap.
key_risks: Synthetic data—real prices may differ significantly
Slippage exceeds synthetic estimate during execution
MEV extraction on Base reduces effective edge

summary: [SYNTHETIC DATA] Suboptimal routing inefficiency detected: WETH→USDC conversion via Aerodrome synthetically 22 bps cheaper than direct Uniswap route. Synthetic scenario assumes liquidity imbalance.
thesis: [SYNTHETIC] Aerodrome's concentrated liquidity positions on Base are synthetically creating temporary price gaps vs. Uniswap for ETH→USDC swaps. A 10 ETH swap shows 22 bps worse execution on Uniswap v3 vs. routing through Aerodrome's AERO-boosted pool (with staked incentives). Conservative edge after costs: ~14 bps.
liquidity_notes: [SYNTHETIC] Assumed ~2M TVL in Aerodrome WETH/USDC pool, 3.5M in Uniswap v3. Synthetic conditions: Aerodrome pool temporarily overweight USDC due to synthetic yield farming skew. Multi-step route: WETH→AERO→USDC or direct through concentrated ticks.
key_risks: Aerodrome incentives subject to rapid change
Bridge risk if cross-DEX route requires additional hops
Liquidity fragmentation risk across multiple protocols
